freeze.content_hash_rule: The freeze envelope records SHA256 for the exact bytes of each reviewed content file and a bundle SHA256 over the ordered path/hash list; the envelope is not included in its own bundle hash.
freeze.rule: Do not create the freeze envelope, record a freeze hash, authorize measurement, or release quarantine until both PIs have approved the draft and the sign-off amendment is committed.

anchor.representation: closed interval [lo_s, hi_s] with source and provenance
anchor.missing_or_unusable_anchor: axis observation is missing for every anchor-dependent axis
anchor.multiple_events: each event receives a stable event_id and its own interval

readout_targets.primary: fork-majority or fork-distribution value from continuations of the current state
readout_targets.separation_rule: primary and secondary targets are stored, selected, scored, and reported separately

categorical_commitment.agreement: sum_c n_c*(n_c-1)/(n*(n-1)) over distinct scorable fork pairs
categorical_commitment.independent_baseline: the same statistic across independent base-seed finals for the event
categorical_commitment.gain: (A_fork-A_ind)/(1-A_ind) only when A_ind is below the numerical video-determined boundary
categorical_commitment.video_determined: A_ind at the numerical identity boundary is a separate status and is excluded from window estimation; the scientific label video-determined additionally requires demonstrated between-video discrimination under the axis calibration gate
categorical_commitment.minimum_support: selected by a power calculation on development data and frozen before pilot outcomes

window_rule.commitment: earliest sampled progress whose lower video-bootstrap confidence bound passes the development-selected commitment criterion and remains passing at all later supported points
window_rule.information_readout: earliest supported progress where the readout lower confidence bound exceeds the correct null and conditioning-only baseline by the frozen development margin
window_rule.action_readout: earliest supported progress additionally meeting the axis-specific accuracy and calibration criterion selected on development data
window_rule.unsupported_grid_points: reported as unsupported, never interpolated into a claim

threshold_and_model_selection.procedure: predeclare each axis candidate grid and its single preference direction; use video-disjoint nested development folds; retain candidates within one standard error of the best primary metric; apply the axis-declared preference; break remaining ties by calibration and then stable lexical order

presence.scientific_meaning: Whether the specified visible event has a corresponding audio event near its anchor; unrelated background audio does not count.
prerequisites: event-centered audio crop policy selected on development data

target_type.continuous: target-event presence score with component scores and confidence

presence.selection_procedure: Predeclare target-semantic, anchor-local temporal, and acoustic-salience component families; fit candidate fusion rules and the present/absent/uncertain decision rule only in video-disjoint development folds; among candidates within one standard error of best balanced accuracy, choose the fewest-component fusion, then highest coverage, then lowest calibration error, then stable lexical ID; freeze before pilot or confirmatory outcomes.
presence.missingness: uncertain is missing evidence and excluded from correctness/agreement denominators; its rate and reason remain mandatory

reference_and_negative.positive: audio with a semantically matched event overlapping the anchor interval
reference_and_negative.negative: event-absent or silent control for the target event
reference_and_negative.hard_negative: unrelated background audio, including salient off-target events, without the target event

presence.commitment_statistic: shared categorical commitment on present/absent with uncertain forks excluded and support reported

external_readout.target: primary and secondary readout targets kept separate

internal_readout.target: fork distribution over present/absent (primary); ODE-final state (secondary)

robustness_tests: gain and loudness perturbations that preserve target-event semantics
off-anchor unrelated-background injection must not become target presence
target-event removal is a positive control and must change the state
presence.semantic_calibration: blinded event-level present/absent/uncertain ratings; reuse existing human/MLLM evidence only as diagnostic context unless exact v2 event joins are materialized

disposition_rules.PASS: all calibration gates pass against target-event controls with adequate support
disposition_rules.UNRESOLVED: insufficient support, calibration, or event-level labels
disposition_rules.INVALID_MEASUREMENT: off-target background systematically counts as presence or the detector fails the correct null
disposition_rules.INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS: required anchors, component scores, controls, or provenance fields are missing
disposition_rules.ENGINEERING_FAILURE: deterministic execution, joins, schemas, or artifact validation fail
disposition_rules.redesign_or_demotion: redesign components/fusion on development data; exclude the axis if failure persists

timing.scientific_meaning: Continuous onset offset of the target audio event relative to the specified visual-event anchor.
timing.selection_procedure: Keep delta_t continuous. On video-disjoint development folds, select onset association, confidence/undefined handling, and any commitment-equivalence tolerance from a predeclared grid using interval-aware median absolute error; among candidates within one standard error of best error, choose highest defined coverage, then the simplest onset association, then stable lexical ID; report fixed 50/100/200 ms readout metrics regardless of the selected commitment tolerance.
timing.missingness: undefined for absent/uncertain presence or missing onset; never coerced to zero or a bin

reference_and_negative.positive: adjudicated target-event onset interval paired to the visual-event anchor
reference_and_negative.negative: zero-offset and video-prior predictions are baselines, not pseudo-labels
reference_and_negative.multi_onset_rule: event identity and anchor overlap determine the onset match; nearest arbitrary clip onset is forbidden

null_and_baseline: video/anchor-position prior learned on development data
timing.commitment_statistic: distinct-pair probability that two defined fork offsets differ by at most the event tolerance; tolerance procedure uses onset and anchor uncertainty on development data and is frozen before pilot outcomes
robustness_tests: gain/EQ/codec perturbations must preserve delta_t within propagated uncertainty
off-anchor background must not replace the target onset
known audio time shift must move delta_t by the injected shift
wider anchor intervals must widen uncertainty rather than alter the point estimate silently
timing.semantic_calibration: blinded target-onset interval marks with event identity; old gross-bin human aggregate is diagnostic only

disposition_rules.PASS: defined cases beat zero-offset and video-prior nulls with calibrated intervals and acceptable support
disposition_rules.UNRESOLVED: too few confidently present events or onset/anchor uncertainty dominates
disposition_rules.INVALID_MEASUREMENT: the detector follows unrelated clip onsets or fails known-shift equivariance
disposition_rules.INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS: required presence, anchor, onset, or provenance records are missing
disposition_rules.ENGINEERING_FAILURE: deterministic execution, joins, schemas, or interval validation fail
disposition_rules.redesign_or_demotion: repair onset-event association on development data; exclude timing if unresolved

class.scientific_meaning: Confident coarse semantic class of the target audio event.
class.unit_of_analysis: event-centered audio region; clip-level continuity output is retained only as an audit lens
class.selection_procedure: Calibrate the full posterior on inner video-disjoint development folds; enumerate a predeclared abstention grid; discard candidates without powered per-class support; among candidates within one standard error of best balanced accuracy choose highest coverage, then lower calibration error, then stable threshold order.
class.missingness: abstain and missing posterior are excluded from confident-subset agreement and accuracy; coverage remains mandatory

reference_and_negative.positive: event-level semantic label under the frozen coarse map
reference_and_negative.negative: other coarse classes in the same frozen universe
reference_and_negative.context_control: off-target/background events are separated by event-centered cropping and calibration labels

class.commitment_statistic: shared categorical commitment on confident coarse classes; abstain support is reported side by side
class.semantic_calibration: reuse the existing human/MLLM class evidence as diagnostic context; v2 PASS requires exact event-level joins or a new blinded package

disposition_rules.PASS: exact v2 reconstruction and semantic calibration pass with powered class support
disposition_rules.UNRESOLVED: event-level semantic calibration or per-class support is insufficient
disposition_rules.INVALID_MEASUREMENT: posterior mapping/cropping fails semantic controls or exact reconstruction reveals a real error
disposition_rules.INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS: full posteriors, event crops, anchors, mapping hashes, or exact joins are missing
disposition_rules.ENGINEERING_FAILURE: deterministic inference, posterior mapping, joins, schemas, or reconstruction code fail
disposition_rules.redesign_or_demotion: preserve the old result as audit history and narrow/rebuild only if v2 evidence requires it

continuity_evidence.status: preserve unless exact v2 reconstruction reveals a real error

material.scientific_meaning: Whether generated target-event material is closer to the same-event material reference than to a matched different-material reference.

target_type.forbidden_conversion: raw cosine is never a Bernoulli correctness probability

material.selection_procedure: Freeze the positive/negative manifest without using candidate margins. On video-disjoint development trials, select embedding preprocessing and an indifference margin from a predeclared grid; among candidates within one standard error of best AUROC choose the simplest preprocessing, then highest repeat-stable coverage, then lowest calibration error against blinded material judgments, then stable lexical ID; freeze before pilot or confirmatory outcomes.
material.missingness: indifferent, missing-reference, failed-match, and zero-norm embeddings are unscorable with separate reason counts

reference_and_negative.positive: same-video, same-event reference from a disjoint generation; leave-one-out when a reference bank is used
reference_and_negative.negative: different-video, same coarse class, similar event timing and loudness, demonstrably different material
reference_and_negative.matching: freeze IDs and matching metadata before measuring candidates; candidate never contributes to either reference
reference_and_negative.easy_negative: allowed only as a labeled sanity check, never the primary estimand

material.commitment_statistic: shared categorical commitment on positive/negative 2AFC decisions with indifferent trials excluded; margin distributions remain primary supporting evidence
material.semantic_calibration: blinded material-similarity/difference judgments for the frozen positive/negative trials; absent ratings require a human-ready package and UNRESOLVED status
material.continuity_task: Resolve FLAG A2-4 on exactly 6,400 existing Phase-2 cells (200 clips x four subjects x eight progress points); 800 subject-final embeddings survive, while candidate previews/embeddings and matched-negative embeddings do not. Freeze positive-reference composition/aggregation/disjointness and the other-video negative manifest, then persist candidate, positive, and negative embeddings.

disposition_rules.PASS: matched 2AFC beats chance and permutation nulls with semantic material calibration and robustness
disposition_rules.UNRESOLVED: missing embeddings/references/ratings or inadequate matched-negative support
disposition_rules.INVALID_MEASUREMENT: performance is driven by class, timing, loudness, or reference leakage rather than material
disposition_rules.INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS: candidate/positive/negative embeddings, auditable reference lineage, or matching fields are missing
disposition_rules.ENGINEERING_FAILURE: deterministic embedding, joins, schemas, leave-one-out validation, or scoring code fail
disposition_rules.redesign_or_demotion: repair matching/representation on development data; exclude material if confounds persist

binding.scientific_meaning: Whether two target audio-event identities are assigned to the corresponding visible-event anchors.
binding.unit_of_analysis: ordered two-event set with two stable event IDs and anchor intervals
prerequisites: separable anchor intervals or an explicit ambiguous status

target_type.primary: correct versus swapped assignment conditional on both target events present

binding.selection_procedure: On video-disjoint development pairs, select event detection, ambiguity, and assignment rules from a predeclared capacity-bounded grid; among candidates within one standard error of best conditional assignment accuracy choose the fewest-component rule, then highest both-present decisive coverage, then stable lexical ID; event-set Presence and temporal-order gates must also pass; freeze before pilot or confirmatory outcomes.
binding.missingness: missing target events and ambiguous assignments are never recoded as swapped; each component retains its own support

reference_and_negative.positive: event identities assigned to their own anchor intervals
reference_and_negative.negative: identity permutation across the two anchors while event set and timing are held fixed

null_and_baseline: assignment chance=0.5 conditional on both events present
binding.commitment_statistic: shared categorical commitment on decisive correct/swapped assignments conditional on both events present; event-set and order commitment are reported separately
robustness_tests: missing-event cases never score as swapped
unrelated extra event changes only the extra-event component
known identity swap is a positive control
binding.semantic_calibration: blinded two-event sound-to-visible-event assignment ratings; unrated packages remain UNRESOLVED
binding.b6_inheritance: The banked B6 WAVs remain analysis-banned here. Before any later measurement, every source/donor clip requires curated event IDs and anchor intervals. Later causal work must use these frozen presence/material/binding semantics and a separately signed release amendment.

disposition_rules.PASS: conditional assignment beats chance with event-presence, order, and semantic-calibration gates passing
disposition_rules.UNRESOLVED: insufficient both-present support, overlapping anchors, or missing semantic ratings
disposition_rules.INVALID_MEASUREMENT: missing events are conflated with swaps or assignment follows timing/order shortcuts
disposition_rules.INCOMPLETE_ARTIFACTS: event IDs, two anchors, references, assignment labels, or provenance are missing
disposition_rules.ENGINEERING_FAILURE: deterministic execution, joins, schemas, permutation checks, or shard validation fail
disposition_rules.redesign_or_demotion: repair the decomposed measurers on development data; exclude binding if assignment remains invalid
